answer: The settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline erroneously joined escrow disbursements to party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id. Since a single tax identifier can represent multiple buyer, seller, and entity roles within a single closing, this join duplicated disbursement records for Florida FNF National Title purchase closings, overstating the reconciliation variance KPI on September 15, 2026.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title from settlement.escrow_disbursement_fact
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id, which matched multiple buyer and seller party roles inside the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements in finance.disbursement_recon_daily
